Water Testing & Analysis

Secure the purity of your operations with NABL-accredited precision. We provide end-to-end monitoring for Drinking Water, Effluent, and Ground Water to ensure total regulatory compliance.

Lab Consultation

What's Included

High-precision aqueous analysis and auditing.

NABL Accredited Effluent & Potable Reports
ETP / STP System Performance Audits
Groundwater & Soil Contamination Profiles
Heavy Metal & Hazardous Chemical Screening

Water-quality risks

Contamination can lead to immediate plant closures and hefty SPCB fines.

01

Sampling Errors

Poor sampling techniques in the field lead to flawed lab results. We use scientific, multi-point collection to ensure data representativeness.

02

ETP Performance

Testing is reactive; optimizing your Effluent Treatment Plant is proactive. We identify why your parameters are over the legal limit.

03

Legal Deadlines

Missing a mandatory CTO/CTE testing cycle creates a liability. We manage your testing calendar so you never miss a deadline.

The Analysis Protocol

A technical path to total water quality oversight.

01

Sampling

  • Scientific Collection
  • Cold-chain Logistics
  • Chain of Custody
02

Lab Analysis

  • NABL Procedures
  • BOD/COD Profiles
  • Heavy Metal Trace
03

Validation

  • Anomaly Mapping

04

Reporting

  • Digital Trends

Analysis Assets

We deliver a full technical suite for your water quality management.

NABL Lab Reports
ETP Performance Audit
Compliance Calendar
Trend Benchmarking

Technical Parameters

Analytical standards for high-precision water quality monitoring.

Regulatory Limits
  • IS 10500:2012 Drinking Water (Essential & Toxic)
  • Effluent (ETP) BOD, COD, TSS, TDS, O&G limits
  • Sewage (STP) Fecal Coliform & Nutrients (N/P)
  • ZLD Verification Mass balance of water & salt recovery
Laboratory Standards
  • ISO/IEC 17025 NABL Accreditation for Chemical & Bio
  • Sampling Protocol IS 3025 & APHA 24th Edition
  • Traceability Digital Chain of Custody (CoC) management
  • Heavy Metals ICP-MS/OES for PPB level detection

Water FAQs

Clarifying the science of water resource management.

Testing frequency is typically dictated by your SPCB Consent to Operate (CTO). Most industrial units require monthly laboratory testing of treated effluent, and continuous online monitoring (OCEMS) for parameters like pH, Flow, BOD, and COD if the unit falls under high-pollution categories.
Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) is a treatment process where all industrial wastewater is recycled and reused, leaving no liquid waste for discharge. It is mandatory for specific highly polluting sectors (Textiles, Distilleries, Tannery, etc.) and in "Critically Polluted Areas" as designated by the CPCB.
Yes. State Pollution Control Boards and environmental auditors only accept reports from laboratories accredited by the National Accreditation Board for Testing and Calibration Laboratories (NABL) or recognized by MoEFCC under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
IS 10500:2012 is the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) standard for drinking water. It specifies the "Acceptable" and "Permissible" limits for various physical, chemical, and bacteriological parameters. Compliance with this standard is mandatory for all workplace drinking water supplies.
NABL Accredited BIS Compliant OCEMS Specialist ZLD Verified
